name: Isambard-AI
operator: University of Bristol & Hewlett Packard Enterprise
oneLiner: UK flagship AI supercomputer and one half of the AIRR.
summary: Isambard-AI opened in July 2025 at the National Composites Centre in Bristol. Built by the University of Bristol and HPE at a cost of £225 million, it uses 5,448 NVIDIA GH200 Grace-Hopper superchips in an HPE Cray EX architecture, delivering approximately 21 AI-exaflops. Its modular design uses zero-carbon electricity and fan-less liquid cooling that cut cooling power by up to 90% and emissions by 72%.

keyFacts.hardware: 5,448 NVIDIA GH200 Grace-Hopper superchips on HPE Cray EX
keyFacts.energy: Zero-carbon electricity and fan-less liquid cooling

access.route: Free to UK researchers and start-ups via UKRI allocation routes run with DSIT.

sources.label: UKRI - Isambard-AI and Dawn AIRR supercomputers: Innovator route
